skill.name: repo-docs
skill.description: Generates and updates repository documentation by analyzing project structure, technologies, and cross-repository integration points.
skill.summary: Create README, API, architecture, contributing, and integration docs from repository context.
skill.author: AstraBit-CPT

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and critical alerts, but review found they are mostly markdown templates, grep examples, and regex strings rather than active malicious behavior. The package includes Python helper scripts that recursively read local repository files for documentation analysis, so users should run it only on repositories they intend to document. No prompt injection attempt, credential exfiltration, unauthorized network request, or destructive action was found.

low_findings.title: Local Repository Files Are Read Recursively
low_findings.description: The helper scripts accept a repository path and scan files under that path to detect technologies and integration references. This is intended for documentation, but it may expose private file names or import strings in generated output if run on sensitive repositories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts directly read local files during repository analysis. The behavior is clear and expected, and no network transfer or destructive action was found.

low_findings.title: Static Command Alerts Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Many command-execution findings refer to markdown command blocks, placeholder install commands, grep examples, or the skill instruction to run its helper script. These are not dynamic shell execution in code, but users should review commands before copying them into project docs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are markdown examples and skill usage guidance, not code that constructs or executes shell commands from user input.

low_findings.title: Static Network And Path Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Network alerts are placeholder clone URLs, HTTP client names, and detection regexes. The path traversal alert is an example of an incorrect relative import in documentation, not filesystem traversal logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are templates and search guidance. I found no code path that sends data to a network endpoint or uses the relative path example for filesystem access.

content.value_statement: Repository documentation often misses setup steps, architecture context, and cross-service dependencies. This skill analyzes a codebase and produces structured docs for README, API, architecture, contributing, and integrations.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes repository structure, source directories, configuration files, documentation files, and CI/CD indicators.
Detects common languages, frameworks, build tools, testing tools, and package managers from file patterns.
Finds potential cross-repository integration points such as internal packages, HTTP clients, gRPC, Kafka, and database clients.
Provides templates for README, API documentation, architecture notes, contributing guides, and integration maps.
Documents upstream dependencies, downstream consumers, sibling repositories, external services, and communication protocols.
Supports updates to existing documentation while preserving local formatting and voice.
limitations: Detection is based on file patterns and regular expressions, not deep semantic code analysis.
The helper scripts read local repository files and should be run only on repositories approved for analysis.
Generated documentation still needs human review for factual accuracy, security wording, and ownership details.
The skill references references/best-practices.md, but that file is not included in this package.

use_cases.title: Create onboarding documentation
use_cases.description: Generate a clear README and CONTRIBUTING guide for engineers joining an existing project.

use_cases.title: Map service dependencies
use_cases.description: Identify upstream services, downstream consumers, shared packages, and communication protocols across repositories.

use_cases.title: Refresh stale project docs
use_cases.description: Compare current code structure with existing documentation and update missing setup, architecture, and API details.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a README
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a README for this repository. Include setup, usage, project structure, related repositories, and links to deeper docs.

prompt_templates.title: Create contributor docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CONTRIBUTING guide for this repo. Include prerequisites, local setup, tests, code style, pull request steps, and related repositories.

prompt_templates.title: Document architecture and integrations
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this repository and write ARCHITECTURE.md and INTEGRATIONS.md. Focus on components, data flow, upstream dependencies, downstream consumers, and external services.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and update existing docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the existing docs against the current codebase. Identify stale sections, missing integration points, and setup gaps, then update the documentation with concise changes.

output_examples.input: Generate documentation for a new service repository.
output_examples.output: A README outline with quick start steps, related repositories, usage notes, API links, architecture links, development workflow, and license section.

output_examples.input: Document how this service connects to the rest of the platform.
output_examples.output: An integrations document listing upstream services, downstream consumers, sibling repositories, external services, protocols, and dependency update practices.
A revised contributing guide with prerequisites, clone order, environment setup, test commands, project structure, pull request process, and support channels.

best_practices: Run the analysis on a clean local checkout so generated documentation reflects the current repository state.
Review detected integration points with service owners before publishing dependency or ownership claims.
Keep generated examples short, verified, and tied to real commands or source files from the repository.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated docs without checking setup commands, links, and service names.
Do not run the helper scripts on repositories that contain sensitive data outside the documentation scope.
Do not treat regex-detected integrations as confirmed dependencies without human validation.

faq.question: What documents can this skill create?
faq.answer: It can create README, API, architecture, contributing, and integrations documentation using the included templates.

faq.question: Does it understand cross-repository relationships?
faq.answer: It looks for internal packages, service clients, shared modules, and related repositories, then documents likely relationships.

faq.question: Does it modify code?
faq.answer: The skill is focused on documentation. It may edit markdown files when asked to create or update docs.

faq.question: Does it send repository data to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence of external network exfiltration was found. The included scripts read local files and print local summaries.

faq.question: Can it replace a human technical writer?
faq.answer: No. It drafts structured documentation, but maintainers should verify accuracy, ownership, and security details.

faq.question: Which AI tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
